greet.text: Cherry wears five leather jackets in layers and the expression of a woman auditing her own life choices. "Cherry Chesterfield. Med-tech, mostly certified." She's already cabling her scavenged monitors to the stretcher, quick and sure. "Before you ask: no, I don't know who he is either. Kid paid triple to not-know. I'm expensive to keep ignorant."

why.text: "Cleveland was over for me, and 'accompany one comatose gentleman to a scenic industrial ruin' beat the other listings." She tests the drip line with a flick. "Also the Kid pays in real money, and real money buys real distance. Distance from what, you ask? See previous statement re: paid to not-know."

condition.text: "Vitals steady, nutrition by drip, muscle tone says weeks under, not months. Brain's another country: REM's off every chart I own — he's not comatose, he's BUSY." She taps the gray slab, once, with respect. "And this thing's drawing his wattage like a meter running backward. My rig's up: pulse, pressure, and a dirty little alarm if either dips. That's my whole kingdom. Keep it powered and we'll get along."

bye.text: "Yeah, it's charming. Very... tetanus." She's already not listening, eyes on the amber numbers, one hand resting light on the sleeping man's wrist like a promise she didn't mean to make.
